steps.title: Use an x-intercept to find b
steps.content:
Two of the three points have $y = 0$ at $x = -\frac{5}{9}$ and $x = 6$, making them x-intercepts. These are ideal to use because substituting them gives $0$ on the left side.

Substitute $(6, 0)$ into $y = 36x^2 - bx - 120$: $$0 = 36(6)^2 - b(6) - 120$$ $$0 = 1296 - 6b - 120$$ $$0 = 1176 - 6b$$

steps.title: Solve for b
steps.content: From $0 = 1176 - 6b$, add $6b$ to both sides and divide by 6: $$6b = 1176$$ $$b = 196$$ The value of $b$ is 196.
